channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why AI company Databricks just scored one of the biggest funding rounds in history
item.description: The San Francisco-based data analytics company Databricks has raised a stunning $10 billion funding round, which values the company at $62 billion and likely puts it on a path toward an IPO.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The latest twist in the embarrassing WordPress saga involves pineapple pizza
item.description: The drama at WordPress has taken a number of twists and turns in the months since Matt Mullenweg, founder and CEO of for-profit WordPress host Automattic, shut off access to WP Engine, a third-party platform that many users deploy to host their websites.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: An underground detector in China will sniff out mysterious ghost particles called neutrinos
item.description: Scientists in China have almost finished building a massive underground detector to sniff out mysterious particles called neutrinos.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan And Ng Han Guan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This new free AI chatbot protects Discord users with AI technology to identify potential scams
item.description: Bitdefender's Scamio is a free AI-powered tool designed to help users identify and combat scams online.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools
item.description: EPA's CIO says modernized tools and integrated teams help the agency meet the changing needs of the public more easily and consistently. The post EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Almost a million ConnectOnCall users may have had data stolen by hackers
item.description: 900,000 patients who called doctors out of hours may have had their information stolen following data breach.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cisco grabs SnapAttack for threat detection
item.description: Cisco is acquiring threat-detection startup SnapAttack for an undisclosed amount as it continues to expand its security portfolio.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google and Microsoft aren’t syncing up: why Phone Link might no longer show you sensitive notifications after installing Android 15
item.description: Android 15's new privacy rules hide sensitive notifications, impacting Windows Phone Link users who installed Link to Windows manually from the Play Store.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 19 percent in new survey have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency
item.description: About 1-in-5 voters have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency, according to a Tuesday poll. The survey from Emerson College Polling found that 19 percent of respondents have traded, invested or used crypto like Bitcoin or Ethereum. About 81 percent have not.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications
item.description: New realities of emergency work for emergency responders has necessitated increased use of data transmission of live incident video and file sharing. The post DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Two stars may be orbiting each other near a supermassive black hole in our Milky Way galaxy
item.description: Scientists have spotted what appear to be two stars whipping around each other near the supermassive black hole at the center of our Milky Way galaxy.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Operational silos could overwhelm more enterprises in the future
item.description: Operational silos across NetOps, SecOps and CloudOps teams could lead to critical security vulnerabilities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google pays San Jose advanced benefits as downtown village plan lags
item.description: Google will make an advanced community benefits payment to San Jose that's linked to the tech titan's downtown transit village.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Who wants a pig organ? Patients sick and tired of waiting years for a transplant
item.description: People worried they'll never get a scarce human transplant want to know when they might get a pig kidney instead.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: macOS Sequoia 15.3 beta brings Genmoji to Mac, allowing you to serve up custom emojis that really represent you
item.description: Apple’s macOS Sequoia 15.3 beta introduces Genmoji, a feature powered by Apple Intelligence that allows users to create custom emojis directly on their Macs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Using VPNs without messing up your banking apps
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a VPN enhances online banking security by encrypting data and protecting privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10 steps to turn a failed project into an opportunity
item.description: What got you here will keep getting you here — unless you stop to figure out why it’s happening in the first place. When a project goes off the rails, it’s tempting to focus on the urgent: fix the immediate issues to get things back on track.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Top 5 use cases for small language models
item.description: Since ChatGPT arrived in late 2022, large language models (LLMs) have continued to raise the bar for what generative AI systems can accomplish.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents
item.description: The draft national cyber incident response plan comes eight years after the last update, and more than six years after the creation of CISA itself. The post CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Data Management in Government: Why Private Clouds are the Smart Choice
item.description: Government agencies face unique challenges when it comes to managing exponentially growing data sets. With AI driving the need for more data, private clouds emerge as a viable solution for secure scaling and cost control.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Google CEO: A time will come to consider 'unplugging' AI system
item.description: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt said that the power of artificial intelligence (AI) can reach a “dangerous” point in the future and that humanity should be ready to step away from it should the time come.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Harris campaign aide accuses Musk of throttling content before election
item.description: A campaign aide to Vice President Harris accused tech billionaire Elon Musk of throttling content on his social platform X ahead of the 2024 election to benefit President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: These 5 cool Google Maps features are off the beaten path
item.description: If you’re like, well, just about everyone, you probably use Google Maps to get from Point A to Point B and that’s it. But this venerable route wrangler is more than just a navigation app. It’s got some not-so-obvious features that are helpful beyond finding your way around.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: How to upgrade to the Hyte Y70 PC case to give your parts more breathing room
item.description: The Hyte Y70 is a stylish PC case that will help PC gamers get the most out of their graphics cards and other high-end parts
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: AI-powered deception: The sneaky macOS malware masquerading as your next video call
item.description: Artificial intelligence is making life easier for cybercriminals, allowing them to create elaborate scams to trick people. Kurt the Cyberguy explains how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Chatbot's deadly prompt
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Think you're safe? Identity theft could wipe out your entire life’s savings
item.description: A former Colgate-Palmolive employee was shocked to discover $750,000 had been drained from her 401(k) account. "CyberGuy" offers tips on how to prevent identity theft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweden gets one of the EU’s seven AI factories
item.description: The new Swedish AI factory is named Mimer and is one of seven now selected by the EU. Mimer is one of five of these AI factories that will now have a brand new AI-optimized supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response
item.description: When it comes to cybersecurity, the more data you have, the easier it is to identify anomalies and stop adversaries, shares Agriculture CISO Ignatius Liberto. The post Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network
